title: cassia exigua
intro: Cassia exigua, also known as the yellow cassia or dwarf cassia, is a small shrub native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Australia and Southeast Asia. Traditionally, the plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Cassia exigua are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. Cassia exigua can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, with the plant's leaves and flowers being used in herbal medicine.
